author: Robert Carr
description: A wheat bread grilled cheese with crisped chorizo or salami, melted cheddar, thin onion, dill pickles, greens, and a small amount of Dijon burger-style sauce.
ingredients: 1 1/2 ounces chorizo, salami, or salchichon-style salami, thinly sliced
A few very thin onion slices
3 to 4 dill pickle slices, patted dry
Small handful baby arugula or lettuce, patted dry
1/2 teaspoon pickle brine from the jar
instructions: Stir the mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, ketchup, and pickle brine together in a small bowl.
Spread the softened butter very thinly on one outside face of each bread slice.
Warm a skillet over medium-low heat for 1 minute.
Add the chorizo or salami and cook for 45 to 60 seconds per side, just until the edges curl and a little fat renders. Transfer to a plate.
Place 1 bread slice mayo-side down in the skillet.
Layer on 1 slice cheddar, the warmed chorizo or salami, the onion slices, and the second slice cheddar.
Close with the second bread slice, mayo-side up.
Cook for 3 to 4 minutes per side, pressing gently with a spatula, until the bread is deep golden and the cheddar is melted.
Open the sandwich carefully, add the pickles, arugula or lettuce, and the Dijon sauce, then close it again.
Rest for 1 minute, then cut in half.
notes: Spicy pecan cold-smoked chorizo pairs especially well with cheddar, pickles, and buttered wheat bread.
Salchichon-style salami also works well. Prosciutto is delicate, so add it after cooking or use it on toast.
The pickles and greens balance the butter, cheddar, and cured meat.
Butter gives the bread a clean toasted flavor; keep the mayonnaise for the sauce.
Medium-low heat is important because wheat sandwich bread browns before cheddar fully melts if the pan is too hot.
If the bread is browned before the cheese melts, lower the heat and cover the skillet for 30 to 60 seconds.
The two slices of cheddar help glue the warmed cured meat and onion into the sandwich.
Add the pickles and greens after cooking so they stay crisp.
title: Chorizo Cheddar Pickle Grilled Cheese
